_meta.mode: POST-CUTOFF HOLDOUT — scored BLIND to forward outcome (T=2026-01-31 > Jan-2026 cutoff)
_meta.scorer: LLM (Claude), reading only <=T PIT fundamentals + filing keyword contexts
_meta.discipline: Authored from scratchpad/holdout_evidence.json (<=T only, NO forward returns) BEFORE running reports/run_holdout.py. Most names are obscure micro-caps with ~zero model priors, so judgment leans entirely on the dumped evidence.
_meta.rubric: same as reports/llm_scores.json (B=earnings 2nd-deriv, C=narrative re-rating, trap_risk structural/cash-burn; ineligible above ceiling 0.70)

scores.note: no-revenue shell (revenue 0 across history, EPS ~-0.04, ocf ~-38k, 2.46M shares flat); 97% drawdown; no business to inflect.
revenue collapsed to 0 (was $20M in 2023), rev_yoy -1.0; biotech-like, $64M cash burning ~$7M/q (~9q runway); already +491% off 52w low so not deeply depressed; no earnings turn.
clear revenue acceleration 83M->140M, rev_yoy +16%->+44%->+69%, accel positive; infrastructure ramp; momentum on; BUT heavy ocf burn (-$85M/q) + leverage typical of infra -> moderate trap.
pre-revenue (rev 14-72k), massive dilution (49M->132M shares in one quarter), deep cash burn; classic story-stock trap.
SPAC-collapse air-mobility: revenue declining 3.9M->1.7M, negative gross margin, reverse-split history (EPS -137), $3.5M cash burning; extreme trap.
micro shell/biotech (rev ~10-200k, accel noise off tiny base), $1.8M cash burning, dilution 2.8M->4.6M; trap.

Flutter: quality large-cap ~46% off high; revenue ~$3.8B growing ~17% (decelerated from 27%), margins flat, $1.7B cash, buyback, profitable-ish (one Q charge -3.91 EPS); no clear 2nd-deriv turn but decisively not a trap.
Cohen & Steers asset manager ~40% off high; revenue steady +6-12%, EPS stable ~0.80 (profitable), clean balance sheet, dividend; modest growth, momentum on; no strong catalyst.
real small turnaround: revenue 10M->16M (rev_yoy +45/+60%), EPS flipped positive 0.17/0.18, inventory destocking, share-repurchase in filing; clean -> low trap.

JBG Smith office REIT: revenue declining 152M->124M (rev_yoy -5 to -17%), secular WFH pressure + leverage; large buyback (84M->59M shares) signals mgmt confidence but no business turn -> moderate trap.
China micro-cap, revenue tiny & declining (300-900k), erratic/negative margins, dilution; 94% drawdown; trap.
Leslie's pools: revenue flat-to-down, heavy LBO leverage, seasonal cash burn (ocf -71 to -105M), reverse split (9.2M->185M->9.3M); levered declining retail -> trap at ceiling.

Willamette Valley Vineyards PREFERRED (yield instrument, not a turnaround equity): revenue flat ~$8-10M, inventory bloating 463->933 days, $0.37M cash, negative ocf; price tracks rates/credit not an inflection -> trap.

TripAdvisor ~53% off high: revenue modestly growing, EPS recovering 0.28/0.43, ~92% margin, $1.2B cash, buyback; filing shows cost-reduction + restructuring + 'accelerating monetization' (Viator) -> genuine narrative, low trap.
micro-cap: gross margin improving 13%->39%, EPS losses narrowing to ~0, ocf turned positive — early operational improvement, BUT revenue declining last 2Q + dilution + $2M cash -> moderate trap.
micro shell: revenue ~0-165k erratic (last Q 0), $24k cash (!), nonsense margins; +200% off low already; trap.
